name: AIRO Group Holdings, Inc.
business: AIRO Group Holdings, Inc. is an aerospace, autonomy, and air mobility platform organized into four segments: Drones, Avionics, Training, and Electric Air Mobility. It develops and sells drones, avionics, and provides military pilot training, and is developing electric and hybrid-electric rotorcraft for cargo and passenger applications. Revenue is primarily from sales to military and defense customers, including NATO countries and the U.S. government, as well as OEMs in the aerospace industry.
sellsTo: OEMs (Robinson Helicopters, Pilatus, Honeywell, Joby Aviation)

exposures.sentence: We are a technologically differentiated aerospace, autonomy, and air mobility platform targeting 21 st century aerospace and defense opportunities.

The Electric Air Mobility segment, operated through our Jaunt brand, is developing dual-use electric and hybrid-electric compound rotorcraft aircraft designed for cargo, government, and passenger applications.
We also serve as an avionics supplier for OEMs, including Robinson Helicopters, Pilatus, Honeywell, and Joby Aviation.
For example, AIRO Drone expects to operate drones in an AI-based commercial inspection service known as DaaS and build and operate a worldwide drone datacom network known as AIRO-NET.

catalysts.sentence: We expect to achieve Blue UAS certification by June 2026.
We are targeting first flight by the end of 2026 and initial aircraft deliveries beginning in the fourth quarter of 2027.

catalysts.event: Initial aircraft deliveries of cargo eVTOL
catalysts.sentence: We are targeting first flight by the end of 2026 and initial aircraft deliveries beginning in the fourth quarter of 2027.

catalysts.event: Certification of cargo eVTOL under drone rules
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate certification of our initial cargo aircraft under applicable unmanned aircraft regulations as early as 2027

catalysts.event: Certification of passenger aircraft under CAR 529
catalysts.sentence: and expect subsequent certification of a multi-role cargo and passenger aircraft by the TCCA under existing CAR 529 Transport Category Rotorcraft airworthiness rules as early as 2031.

tamClaims.claim: Combined total addressable market for all four segments estimated to be over $315.4 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: These four segments collectively target a combined total addressable market estimated to be over $315.4 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Military drone market size expected to reach approximately $24.75 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The military drone market size is expected to reach approximately $24.75 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Worldwide commercial drone revenues will reach $163.5 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: It is anticipated that worldwide commercial drone revenues will reach $163.5 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Commercial training market projected to grow from $1.8 billion in 2023 to over $4.9 billion in 2030, CAGR of 15.4%.
tamClaims.sentence: The commercial training market is projected to grow from $1.8 billion in 2023 to over $4.9 billion in 2030, representing a CAGR of 15.4%.

tamClaims.claim: Global electric air mobility market may grow to approximately $55 billion by 2030 and to approximately $1 trillion by 2040.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that the global electric air mobility market may grow to approximately $55 billion by 2030 and to approximately $1 trillion by 2040.
